anchors.title: Task-specific substance beats polish
anchors.decision_rule: Prefer the paper that more fully answers the assignment with developed ideas and required support, even when the other paper is smoother.

anchors.title: Polish matters when substance is close
anchors.decision_rule: Use organization, style, and conventions as tie-breakers once task fulfillment, idea quality, and support are comparable.

anchors.title: Completion can be decisive
anchors.decision_rule: A complete but weak response usually outranks an unfinished scaffold or fragment unless the fragment contains substantially stronger required work.

caution_checks: Is one paper winning only because it is longer, cleaner, or more formulaic?
Does either paper fail the core genre purpose even if the prose sounds fluent?
Do surface errors actually block meaning, or are they secondary to stronger content?
